summary.intent: Implement fix for AGENTS.md and TOOLS.md silent truncation (issue #593) by making the limit configurable, raising the default from 6K to 50K chars, adding warn logging on truncation, and surfacing truncation metadata in the full_context API response.
summary.outcome: All five planned files were modified, 3 new unit tests added, PR #610 created and iterated on to resolve code review comments. The fix was committed and pushed to the stealth-harp branch.

repo: WORKSPACE_FILE_MAX_CHARS was hardcoded at 6,000 in crates/agents/src/prompt.rs with no logging, while SOUL.md had no limit — inconsistent design.
Config schema changes require updates in three files: schema.rs (struct field + default fn), validate.rs (build_schema_map Leaf entry), and template.rs (commented example line).
Semantic validation warnings for numeric config values follow a pattern already used for agent_max_iterations — zero-value guard via check_semantic_warnings.
tracing is a regular dependency in moltis-agents (not feature-gated), so warn!() can be used directly.
llama-cpp-sys-2 requires cmake/CUDA and causes pre-existing clippy build failures unrelated to application code — run clippy scoped to affected crates only.
The project uses a beads CLI tool for issue claiming and closing.
PromptRuntimeContext does not derive Default; all struct literal constructions (including ~12 test instances) must be updated when adding new fields.

code.finding: Original WORKSPACE_FILE_MAX_CHARS = 6_000 const replaced with DEFAULT_WORKSPACE_FILE_MAX_CHARS = 50_000

append_truncated_text_block calls truncate_prompt_text internally — callers pre-counting chars().count() caused a double O(n) traversal; fixed by making truncate_prompt_text return (String, bool) truncation flag
Secondary caller of truncate_prompt_text needed updating to destructure the (String, bool) tuple after return type change
full_context() RPC response extended with workspaceFiles array containing per-file truncation metadata (file, chars, limit, truncated)
build_schema_map chat struct requires explicit Leaf entry for each new ChatConfig field

workflow: Greptile automated review caught the duplicated magic constant 50_000 in chat/src/lib.rs; resolved by using ChatConfig::default().workspace_file_max_chars as the fallback instead.
Running cargo check/test scoped to specific crates (moltis-agents, moltis-config) is faster and avoids unrelated build failures from native dependencies like llama-cpp-sys-2.
replace_all on Edit tool is efficient for updating many identical struct field patterns (e.g., adding workspace_file_max_chars: None after nodes: None across 12 test instances).
PR review comments from Greptile appeared as issue body text updates rather than inline review comments, requiring gh api calls to detect.

friction: llama-cpp-sys-2 cmake/CUDA build failure caused `just lint` to appear to fail; required scoping clippy to specific crates to confirm changes were clean.
PromptRuntimeContext having 12+ explicit test constructions meant adding a new field required a bulk replace across all test instances.
Greptile review feedback appeared in the PR body text rather than as inline review comments, making it harder to detect and resolve programmatically.
Changing truncate_prompt_text return type from String to (String, bool) caused a compilation failure at a secondary call site that needed a separate fix.
open_items: Web UI indicator on Settings > Agent page showing AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md size and truncation status — requires JS changes in page-agents.js and a new RPC endpoint or gon data (deferred as separate PR).
Dynamic scaling of workspace_file_max_chars based on model context window size — requires model to be resolved before prompt construction, changing call order (deferred as follow-up).
No lower-bound enforcement for workspace_file_max_chars = 0 beyond a semantic warning — a hard validation error might be more appropriate.
